jobs.title: Endpoint Engineer
jobs.summary: ABOUT THE JOB The ACLU seeks applicants for the full-time position of Endpoint Engineer in the Information Technology Department of the ACLU’s National office in New York, NY. This is a hybrid role that has in-office requirements of four (4) days per week o...
jobs.description:
ABOUT THE JOB
The ACLU seeks applicants for the full-time position of Endpoint Engineer in the Information Technology Department of the ACLU’s National office in New York, NY. This is a hybrid role that has in-office requirements of four (4) days per week or sixteen (16) days per month.

The Information Technology (IT) department provides technology-based services and support for a more than 600-person National staff at our New York, Washington D.C., San Francisco, the Dakotas, Puerto Rico and Durham offices, including staff based in remote locations, and Affiliate offices nationwide that participate in the Affiliate Technology Services program. ACLU IT support staff ensures that business critical services are available to all staff.

This position is part of a collective bargaining unit. It is represented by ACLU Staff United (ASU).

WHAT YOU'LL DO
Reporting to the Director of Service Desk Operations and End-User Experience, the Endpoint Engineer is responsible for the deployment, configuration, security, and proactive management of end-user computing devices including: desktops, laptops, mobile devices, and peripherals. This role ensures endpoints are secure, compliant, reliable, and optimized to support business operations.

YOUR DAY TO DAY

Proactively manage, maintain, and provide exceptional and timely technical support for ACLU-issued and Bring-Your-Own-Device (BYOD) endpoint devices across the organization. These include but are not limited to Microsoft Windows, Apple MacOS/iOS/iPadOS, and Android
Deploy and manage endpoint management solutions, e.g., Microsoft Intune for Mobile Device Management (MDM) and Mobile Application Management (MAM), Ninja One, JAMF
Working with the Infrastructure team, appropriately size, deploy, proactively manage and monitor virtual desktop (VDs), cloud PCs, i.e., Microsoft Azure VD and Microsoft 365
Manage endpoint security tools (MAM, EDR, antivirus, disk encryption, DLP)
Configure device policies, profiles, compliance rules, and application deployments
Monitor endpoint health, performance, and security posture
Implement OS imaging, provisioning, patching, and update strategies
Troubleshoot endpoint-related issues escalated from the Service Desk
Maintain inventory of devices, software, and licenses Support endpoint lifecycle management (onboarding / enrollment, offboarding, refresh, and secure disposal)
Collaborate with Information Security, Infrastructure, Network Operations, and Service Desk teams to ensure operational best practices, coordinated technical service delivery, issue resolutions, and remediation strategies deployment
Develop and maintain documentation, standards, and procedures
Assist with audits and ensure compliance with company and regulatory requirements

FUTURE ACLU'ERS WILL

Be committed to advancing the mission of the ACLU
Center and embed the principles of equity, inclusion and belonging in their work by demonstrating commitment to diversity with an approach that respects and values multiple perspectives
Be committed to work collaboratively and respectfully toward resolving obstacles and conflicts

WHAT YOU'LL BRING

Extensive experience in endpoint administration or desktop engineering
Expert knowledge of Windows and MacOS operating platforms including Microsoft Windows 11 and Office 365, MacOS, and Mobile technologies
Adept at problem-solving, troubleshooting desktop, cloud PC, and mobile device issues, effectively assisting onsite and remote users to resolve issues, involving other members of technical staff as necessary
Excellent and adaptable communication and documentation skills for technical and non-technical audiences
Expert knowledge of MDM/MAM tools, i.e., Intune, SCCM, Ninja One, Apple Business Manager
Experience sizing, deploying, managing, monitoring Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) and Microsoft 365
Solid understanding and experience with endpoint security principles and best practices
Experienced writing, debugging, and deploying scripts and automation tools, e.g., PowerShell, Python, Power Automate, and using code management tools, e.g., GitHub
Strong knowledge of and experience with Azure AD, Entra ID, identity and access management (IAM) leveraging least privilege principles
Demonstrated ability to work independently and as part of a team
Must have strong, reliable home internet service when working remotely
Must be able to transport moderately heavy objects, e.g., computers and peripherals
Experience with Zero Trust and modern device management strategies
Knowledge of and experience with networking fundamentals (DNS, DHCP, VPN)
Experience supporting remote and hybrid workforces
Have attained or currently enrolled in training towards obtaining professional industry certifications within six (6) months of employment: Microsoft Endpoint Administrator Associate, JAMF Certified Admin, CompTIA A+, Network+, Security+

COMPENSATION
The ACLU is committed to equity, transparency, and clarity in pay. Consistent with our compensation philosophy, there is a set salary for each role based on geographic work location. The annual salary for this position is $128,294 (Level G), reflecting the salary of a position based in New York, NY. Salaries are subject to a regional pay adjustment if authorization is granted to work outside of the location listed in this posting.

For details on our pay structure, please visit: https://www.aclu.org/careers/ACLU_Geographic_Pay_Structure-July_2024.pdf
WHY THE ACLU
For over 100 years, the ACLU has worked to defend and preserve the individual rights and liberties guaranteed by the Constitution and laws of the United States. Whether it’s ending mass incarceration, achieving full equality for the LGBTQ+ community, establishing new privacy protections for our digital age, or preserving the right to vote or the right to have an abortion, the ACLU takes up the toughest civil liberties cases and issues to defend all people.

We know that great people make a great organization. We value our people and know that what we offer is essential not just their work, but to their overall well-being.

At the ACLU, we offer a broad range of benefits, which include:

Time away to focus on the things that matter with a generous paid time-off policy
Focus on your well-being with comprehensive healthcare benefits (including medical, dental and vision coverage, parental leave, gender affirming care & fertility treatment)
Plan for your retirement with 401k plan and employer match
We support employee growth and development through annual professional development funds, internal professional development programs and workshops

OUR COMMITMENT TO ACCESSIBILITY, EQUITY, DIVERSITY & INCLUSION
Accessibility, equity, diversity and inclusion are core values of the ACLU and central to our work to advance liberty, equality, and justice for all. For us diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion are not just check-the-box activities, but a chance for us to make long-term meaningful change. We are a community committed to learning and growth, humility and grace, transparency and accountability. We believe in a collective responsibility to create a culture of belonging for all people within our organization – one that respects and embraces difference; treats everyone equitably; and empowers our colleagues to do the best work possible. We are as committed to anti-oppression, anti-ableism, and anti-racism internally as we are externally. Because whether we’re in the courts or in the office, we believe ‘We the People’ means all of us.

With this commitment in mind, we strongly encourage applications from all qualified individuals without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national origin, marital status, citizenship, disability, veteran status and record of arrest or conviction, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

The ACLU is committed to providing reasonable accommodation to individuals with disabilities. If you are a qualified individual with a disability and need assistance applying online, please email benefits.hrdept@aclu.org. If you are selected for an interview, you will receive additional information regarding how to request an accommodation for the interview process.

The Department of Education has determined that employment in this position at the ACLU does not qualify for the Public Service Loan Forgiveness Program.

jobs.description:
Job Title: Endpoint Engineer III

Job Category: Information Technology

Time Type: Full time

Minimum Clearance Required to Start: TS/SCI

Employee Type: Regular

Percentage of Travel Required: Up to 10%

Type of Travel: Local

* * *

The Opportunity:
TCS is a long-term, CACI Prime contract with the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) in support of the customer’s GEOINT mission. The Endpoint Security Engineer will support our Intelligence Community customer’s various endpoint security platforms. Positions on TCS require a TS/SCI clearance to be eligible for hire; with the ability to successfully pass a government polygraph as a post-hire requirement.

Responsibilities:
• Implement and apply technologies, processes, and practices designed to protect networks, devices, programs, and data from malicious attack, damage, or unauthorized access.
• Provide engineering and sustainment support for the various endpoint security solutions.
• Establish computing environment by designing system configuration, directing system installation, and defining, documenting, and enforcing system standards.
• Provide specific, detailed information for hardware and software selection, implementation techniques, and tools for the most efficient solution to meet business needs, including present and future capacity requirements.
• Work with both the software vendor and the customer to research, analyze, and implement enterprise-wide security solutions/capabilities/enhancements to support customer business/mission goals and objectives.
• Assist with design, analysis, testing and implementation of state-of-the-art secure architectures.
• Oversee security infrastructure sustainment, maintenance, and advanced configuration (policy management, database administration and security management tools.)
• Oversee the reporting, documentation and investigation of all security related incidents; lead the development and implementation of corrective measures.

Qualifications:

Required:
• Active TS/SCI clearance (As a post-hire requirement, selected candidate must be able to successfully pass and maintain a government polygraph)

• Bachelor's degree (Engineering, Computer Science or related field)

• Minimum 5 years of related experience.

• Active DoD 8140/8570 IAT Level II certified
• Must obtain a CSSP Infrastructure Support certification within 120 days of hire date if one is not currently held. i.e CEH, CySA+
• Strong working knowledge of administering applications in a Windows environment.
• Working knowledge of configuring, deploying, and maintaining Enterprise Cyber Security Engineering requirements utilizing the following Trellix Products: Endpoint Security (ENS), Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Policy Auditor, File Integrity Monitor (FIM) and Application Whitelisting.
• Experience with large-scale enterprise/global networks in a high-paced, diverse environment.
• Understanding of and experience with the DoD Architecture Framework and other key DoD network architecture and strategic planning instructions.
• Experience in providing status reports and products to senior management and customers.
• Experience with plans, designs, and evaluations of security systems and architectures.

-

What You Can Expect:

A culture of integrity.

At CACI, we place character and innovation at the center of everything we do. As a valued team member, you’ll be part of a high-performing group dedicated to our customer’s missions and driven by a higher purpose – to ensure the safety of our nation.

An environment of trust.

CACI values the unique contributions that every employee brings to our company and our customers - every day. You’ll have the autonomy to take the time you need through a unique flexible time off benefit and have access to robust learning resources to make your ambitions a reality.

A focus on continuous growth.

Together, we will advance our nation's most critical missions, build on our lengthy track record of business success, and find opportunities to break new ground — in your career and in our legacy.

Pay Range:

There are a host of factors that can influence final salary including, but not limited to, geographic location, Federal Government contract labor categories and contract wage rates, relevant prior work experience, specific skills and competencies, education, and certifications. Our employees value the flexibility at CACI that allows them to balance quality work and their personal lives. We offer competitive compensation, benefits and learning and development opportunities. Our broad and competitive mix of benefits options is designed to support and protect employees and their families. At CACI, you will receive comprehensive benefits such as; healthcare, wellness, financial, retirement, family support, continuing education, and time off benefits.

The proposed salary range for this position is:

$86,600 - $181,800

CACI is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, age, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other protected characteristic.

jobs.title: Desktop Engineer - Windows
jobs.summary: Computer World Services Corp (CWS) is seeking a detail-oriented and results-driven Desktop Engineer – Windows who is responsible for engineering, configuring, and maintaining Windows-based desktop environments in an enterprise setting. This role performs ad...
jobs.description:
Computer World Services Corp (CWS) is seeking a detail-oriented and results-driven Desktop Engineer – Windows who is responsible for engineering, configuring, and maintaining Windows-based desktop environments in an enterprise setting. This role performs advanced system administration activities, including system configuration, performance optimization, and capacity planning for client systems.

The position ensures reliable, secure, and efficient operation of Windows endpoints, supports enterprise applications, and provides technical expertise to improve system performance and user experience. The Desktop Engineer serves as a key technical resource supporting both end users and IT operations teams.

\n

Key Tasks & ResponsibilitiesSystem Administration & Engineering

Perform daily configuration, administration, and operation of Windows-based desktop and client/server systems.
Engineer, build, and maintain standardized Windows desktop images and configurations.
Optimize system performance, resource utilization, and endpoint stability.
Conduct system capacity analysis and planning to support enterprise desktop environments.
Implement and maintain system configurations in accordance with organizational standards and policies.
Provide Tier 3 support for complex Windows system issues.
Troubleshoot advanced hardware, software, operating system, and performance issues.
Support enterprise applications and system integrations within the Windows environment.
Provide root cause analysis and implement long-term solutions to recurring issues.

Desktop Engineering & Endpoint Management

Design, deploy, and manage Windows desktop environments using enterprise tools (e.g., SCCM, Intune, or equivalent).
Develop and maintain deployment packages, scripts, and automation processes.
Manage patching, updates, and configuration baselines for Windows endpoints.
Ensure endpoint compliance with security and configuration standards.
Support application packaging, testing, and deployment.

User Support & System Access

Provide assistance to users in accessing and effectively using business systems.
Support identity and access management processes (e.g., Active Directory, group policies).
Ensure proper configuration of user environments and system access controls.
Collaborate with support teams to improve user experience and system usability.

System Monitoring & Maintenance

Monitor system performance, logs, and alerts to ensure optimal operation.
Perform proactive maintenance, including patching, updates, and system health checks.
Support backup, recovery, and system resilience processes as required.
Ensure compliance with security policies, standards, and regulatory requirements.

Documentation & Reporting

Develop and maintain documentation for system configurations, processes, and procedures.
Document engineering standards, deployment processes, and troubleshooting guides.
Prepare reports related to system performance, utilization, and capacity planning.
Ensure adherence to change management and configuration management processes.

Collaboration & Support

Work closely with system administrators, network engineers, security teams, and Help Desk staff.
Support enterprise deployments, upgrades, and infrastructure projects.
Provide technical leadership and guidance to Tier 1 and Tier 2 support personnel.
Participate in continuous improvement initiatives and technology evaluations.

Required Skills & Competencies

Advanced knowledge of Windows operating systems (desktop and enterprise environments)
Experience with endpoint management tools (e.g., SCCM, Intune)
Strong understanding of system administration, configuration management, and automation
Experience with Active Directory, Group Policy, and enterprise applications
Ability to optimize system performance and conduct capacity planning
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and troubleshooting skills
Excellent communication and customer service skills

Preferred Qualifications

Experience with scripting (e.g., PowerShell)
Experience in federal or government IT environments

Work Environment

Office and/or customer-site environment
May require on-call support or after-hours maintenance activities
May involve physical handling of IT equipment

Education & Experience
Education

Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field
(or equivalent experience)

Experience

5+ years of experience in desktop engineering, system administration, or enterprise IT support
Experience supporting Windows operating systems in client/server environments
Experience with endpoint management and deployment tools

Certifications

Microsoft certifications (e.g., Modern Desktop Administrator, Azure certifications)
ITIL certification preferred.

Security Clearance
Applicants must be able to obtain a Public Trust clearance

\n

Computer World Services is an affirmative action and equal employment opportunity employer. Current employees and/or qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, genetic information or any other characteristic protected by local, state, or federal laws, rules, or regulations.

Computer World Services is committed to the full inclusion of all qualified individuals. As part of this commitment, Computer World Services will ensure that individuals with disabilities (IWD) are provided reasonable accommodations. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please contact Human Resources at hr@cwsc.com.

jobs.description:
ACS Desktop Workstations Windows System Engineer

Description -

The Systems Engineer for ACS Desktop Workstations is responsible for defining, integrating, and validating the end‑to‑end Windows software solution for new workstation platforms. This role owns system-level design and integration across Windows OS, drivers, firmware, HP applications, recovery solutions, and PAN/PS-delivered components.

This role represents the Windows software stack within the platform team, ensuring a cohesive, high-quality workstation experience across the NPI and refresh lifecycles.

Key Responsibilities:

Windows NPI Program Ownership

Drive system architecture, integration, and interface alignment across platform components

Ensure seamless integration of Windows OS, drivers, HP applications, imaging, and recovery solutions

Lead system-level validation, debug, and root cause analysis to ensure quality and performance

Partner with engineering, ODMs, and factory teams to deliver a robust, production-ready system

Define and validate release readiness criteria from a full system perspective

Cross‑Functional Technical Leadership

Serve as the Windows SEPM representative on the system team and R&D Program Alignment Team, coordinating with:

Program management

OS Engineering (Windows)

BIOS/FW teams

Validation and Quality

Factory, PIN, and Supply Chain

ODM program management and engineering team

Lead technical alignment and decision‑making across stakeholders to resolve integration gaps and risks.

Issue Management & Escalation

Own Windows‑related issue resolution/triage, prioritization, tracking, and closure for NPI.

Host and drive tiger teams for critical, blocking, aging, or bounce issues.

Perform risk assessment and escalate program‑level concerns to management with clear mitigation plans.

Release & RTM Execution

Ensure completion of Windows validation and readiness for:

Image RTM/Release

Web packages and Windows Update readiness

Factory and recovery media enablement

Drive Windows RTM communications and ensure downstream alignment across HP.com, factory, and service channels.

ODM & Factory Enablement

Writes and executes complete testing plans, protocols, and documentation for assigned portion of application

Coordinate closely with ODM SEPMs to ensure Windows deliverables meet HP quality and schedule targets.

Support factory ramp, production line issues, and early‑life field escalations related to Windows software.

Ensure smooth transition from NPI to sustaining ownership post‑RTM.

Status, Communication & Reporting

Consolidate and communicate Windows program status to R&D program team and system leadership and functional stakeholders.

Maintain clear visibility of risks, dependencies, and recovery plans.

Support phase exit readiness reviews and executive updates.

Required Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or equivalent experience.

5+ years in technical program management, systems engineering, or software program leadership.

Strong understanding of Windows OS integration, drivers, BIOS/FW interaction, and system validation.

Experience working with ODMs and factory partners in NPI environments.

Proven ability to drive complex, cross‑functional programs to on‑time delivery.

Preferred Qualifications:

Desktop or workstation platform experience.

Familiarity with Windows image build processes including Windows ADK, driver qualification, and factory readiness.

Ability to read, understand, and write simple Windows scripts.

Experience managing RTM, posting drivers for customer use, and sustaining release flows.

Strong risk management, communication, and executive presentation skills.

Success Indicators

On‑time Windows RTM aligned with system launch.

Reduced late‑stage integration risk and escalations.

Clear ownership and accountability across HP and ODM teams.

Predictable, well‑communicated Windows software readiness for NPI platforms.

Disclaimer

The pay range for this role is $105,050 to $161,800 USD annually with additional opportunities for pay in the form of bonus and/or equity (applies to United States of America candidates only). Pay varies by work location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.

jobs.description:
Amentum is seeking a dedicated Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) Systems Engineer supporting US Army Pacific (USARPAC) at Fort Shafter, Hawaii.

Your focused and resourceful nature keeps our systems running. You thrive in handling the development, integration, operations, and maintenance of our enterprise virtual desktop infrastructure environment, focused on the Army’s Unified Capabilities Architecture. You have extensive experience in VDI Infrastructure, utilizing VMWare, Citrix, and Cisco Hyperconverged technologies. You’re the one our team can depend on to make sure everything is running smoothly. You will also enjoy working independently while also ultimately being part of a larger team.

Responsibilities:

Provide engineering for virtual and physical enterprise servers, and operating, maintaining, and sustaining an enterprise virtual desktop infrastructure environment.
Provide Tier 2/3 Operations and Maintenance (O&M) and engineering support for monitoring and management of day-to-day operations of the VDI environment, including management of Windows Server 2022, Active Directory, IIS (Internet Information Services), Linux Operating Systems (Red Hat and Photon), IPTV, licensing files, routing tables, PKI (Public Key Infrastructure), backups, and firewall configurations. Monitoring of system operability through various logs, including log, admin, and cluster to ensure proper functionality is maintained within the enterprise.
Provide guidance and network administration on configurations and perform diagnostics on the building out and maintain the VDI infrastructure
Coordinate Capability Request (CAPR) work orders to the 30th Network Enterprise Center (NEC) for all IP Management. Provisioning of IP Address space Sub netting, when required.
Implement new systems hardware and software and develops operating procedures, monitors the ongoing operation of a complex VDI environment.

Here at Amentum, our people are supporting some of the most complex government, defense, and intelligence projects across the country and bring innovation and experience to our mission partners. We believe in fostering a sense of belonging. We welcome diversity, encourage growth and development, promote safety first, and believe curiosity is the spark for innovation. We take care of our employees who in turn take care of our customers, driving the success of our business

Minimum Requirements:

Bachelor's Degree (or equivalent work experience)
At least 7 years of recent experience working within the Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) Environment, supporting integration and Operations and Maintenance (O&M)
Current Security+ certification (or be willing to obtain within 3 months of hire)
Knowledgeable and capable of performing all work necessary to plan, design, develop, document, test, implement, integrate, maintain, and/or coordinate modification of the Virtual Desktop environment in conjunction with the Army approved Enterprise Architecture, at the USARPAC C2F and other USARPAC Staff locations
Experience managing IT projects
Must have an active TS clearance and be SCI eligible. Note: U.S. Citizenship is required to obtain a TS Clearance.

jobs.description: Overview GovCIO is currently hiring for a Desktop Systems Engineer 3 for our NLM proposal. The Desktop Systems Engineer 3 performs as a technical specialist and diagnostician responsible for troubleshooting, problem solving, and maintaining corporate desktops and other small systems. This opportunity is a remote opportunity. Responsibilities Responsible for all aspects of small systems configuration, performance, and operation, with strong technical experience that includes maintenance, repair, upgrades, enhancements, and configuration of hardware, software and operating systems in an inter-networked environment. Experienced with desktop standards, desktop management, software deployment, systems administration, and data integrity and recovery. Broad direct contact and work with users of all levels, IT Service Center staff, network and other technical staff requires excellent interpersonal and written English communication skills. Projects may include participation in forming or reviewing desktop/network plans, strategies, and analysis. Participates in and may lead efforts to evaluate, test and introduce new technology and approaches. Plans and executes tasks in conjunction with other system managers and Government staff. Reviews, maintains and enforces approved procedures and quality standards. Qualifications Bachelor\'s with 5 - 8 years (or commensurate experience). Experience in both Microsoft and Apple environments is a plus. They shall demonstrate experience with the installation, configuration and use of a wide range of COTS desktop and network software, and COTS desktop hardware being used at NLM. Certifications: A+ certification is required. An MCSA is required. An MCP/MTA is required. An MCSE is acceptable in lieu of an MCSA. jobs.description:
Overview

About Susquehanna

Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm founded by a group of friends who share a passion for game theory and probabilistic thinking. We have incorporated this approach into our culture, where you will find relentless problem solvers within each of our core disciplines: Trading, Technology, and Quantitative Research. From offices around the world, our employees collaborate to make optimal decisions and are driven by the desire to achieve winning results together.

What we do

We are experts in trading essentially all listed financial products and asset classes, with a focus on derivatives trading. Through market making and market taking, we handle millions of trading transactions around the world every day, providing liquidity and ensuring competitive prices for buyers and sellers. While our presence in the market is broad, our trading desks are highly specialized, allowing for a deep understanding of unique drivers of each asset class.

Team Overview:

Our Platform Services team is an integral part of the Compute and Storage Platforms (CASP) team at Susquehanna and shares responsibility for building and managing all 15,000+ servers and 250+ PB of storage that composes our critical trade focused infrastructure. Joining Platform Services is the first step in building an engineering career with Susquehanna and provides you with opportunities to:

Rotate across technology spaces

As part of the team, you will be able to rotate with senior engineers specializing in Windows, Linux, Virtualization, Storage, and open source technologies such as Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, Ansible, and Grafana. During these rotations you will partner with engineers to learn the fundamentals of their spaces and help them solve complex problems from building out new environments, evaluating and creating new applications, performance tuning, automation, and configuration management.

Work with mentors

In each rotation you will be paired with one or more mentors that will guide you through your technical growth and Susquehanna's environment. In addition to dealing with day-to-day project work, together you will pinpoint your areas of interest and avenues for growth. You will also work closely with the rest of our teams through design/white board sessions, problem solving discussions, and ongoing project and team updates.

Address and solve challenging technical problems

At Susquehanna we want your help to solve challenging problems! Partner with technologists across Susquehanna's environment to troubleshoot application, network, hardware, and other issues while learning and debating the best solution to fit the scenario. You will have the freedom to test and implement your solutions at scale in our onsite labs consisting of over 600 physical and virtual servers.

Engage with bleeding edge technology

Susquehanna is always looking at the latest technology to see where we can increase speed and performance for competitive advantage while managing risk and enhancing stability. You may have the opportunity to help test out new hardware that isn’t generally available (server hardware, network cards, flash storage), work on emerging platforms geared toward container orchestration (Kubernetes, Service Fabric), or work on high performance clustered environments responsible for driving our quantitative trading.

The shift for this role rotates daily between three shifts: 7:00am-4:00pm(Production Lead), 9:00am-6:00pm and 11:00am-8:00pm(Late Support). There is one Production Lead and Late Support per day and the rest of the team works 9:00am-6:00pm. Applicants must be willing to work these shifts

What we're looking for

What we’re looking for:

BS in Information Technology, Computer Science or Engineering, or a similar technical major.Knowledge of computer architecture, OS and network concepts.A passion for technology, learning, and finding creative ways to solve hard problems.Strong personal initiative and excellent communication skills.Experience with any of the following a plus: Python, PowerShell, Bash, Linux, Windows, building computer systems or networks.Visa sponsorship is available for this position.

If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

Job Title: Infrastructure Engineer/Administrator

Job Category: Information Technology

* * *

The Opportunity:

The Infrastructure Engineer / Administrator is responsible for secure operations and modernization across classified and unclassified environments. This role administers Linux and Windows systems, supports enterprise logging and compliance needs, and helps plan and execute mission system migrations into authorized government cloud platforms. The engineer collaborates closely with Network Engineering, Cybersecurity, and mission system owners to ensure high availability, strong security posture, and effective incident response—using vendor‑neutral approaches that accommodate current and future enterprise services.

Responsibilities

* Administer and maintain Linux and Windows systems across both classified and unclassified networks, ensuring secure, stable, and compliant operations
* Support onboarding of system and application logs to the authorized enterprise security logging and monitoring service by ensuring proper log generation, formatting, routing, and ingestion; collaborate with enterprise platform owners to troubleshoot data‑flow issues and maintain compliance with auditing and incident‑response requirements
* Responsibilities may expand to include contributing to the administration or enhancement of the enterprise SIEM platform based on government direction
* Partner with the Network Engineering Team to enable reliable delivery of logs, assist with ingestion workflows, monitor data flows, and troubleshoot aggregation and retrieval issues end‑to‑end
* Perform routine monitoring, patching, configuration updates, and vulnerability remediation to maintain system health, reduce risk, and ensure continuous compliance with required security controls
* Interpret vulnerability scan results, coordinate remediation activities, and document corrective actions to support ongoing risk mitigation
* Provide direct support to Authorization to Operate (ATO) efforts by maintaining system documentation, preparing compliance evidence, tracking security control implementations, and ensuring audit readiness
* Manage centralized logging/monitoring pipelines to maintain data integrity and availability; ensure instrumentation supports auditing and incident investigations
* Plan upgrades and decommissioning for end‑of‑life systems, ensuring proper handling of configurations and data
* Support planning and execution of cloud migrations from hybrid/on‑prem environments into authorized government cloud platforms; configure cloud components including virtual networks, machine images, access controls, and resource policies
* Support cloud account provisioning by creating, configuring, and securing user accounts, roles, and permissions
* Maintain knowledge of cloud logging, auditing, and activity‑tracking capabilities to support compliance monitoring, operational oversight, and incident investigations
* Provide support for data storage and relational database environments including configuration, performance tuning, integration, and backup/restore operations
* Collaborate closely with cross‑functional teams—including network engineers, system owners, developers, and cybersecurity personnel—to support integration, troubleshooting, and modernization of mission systems.

Qualifications

Required:

* Active TS/SCI with Poly clearance
* Bachelor’s degree in computer science, IT, Engineering, or equivalent experience
* Experience administering Linux and Windows in multi‑network secure environments
* Experience supporting enterprise security logging/SIEM onboarding (platform‑agnostic)
* Hands‑on practice with vulnerability management, patching, and configuration baselines
* Experience with cloud systems administration including virtual networks, images, identity/permissions, and compliance
* Proficiency with scripting languages such as Bash and Python, and automation tools
* Experience implementing DevOps tools such as Git and CI/CD pipelines
* Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate with technical and non‑technical personnel
* Must remain on the client site during Program core hours of 0900-1500.

Desired:

* Security+ or DoD 8570 IAT Level II certification
* AWS or Azure certification
* ITIL v4 certification
* Experience supporting SIEM/log analytics onboarding and log source integration
* Experience with network services such as DNS, DHCP, and certificates
* Experience with database backup/restore and performance tuning.

Overview

The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer is responsible for overseeing the organization's end-user computing environment, ensuring the efficient operation, security, and optimization of desktop, laptop, and virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI). This role involves advanced troubleshooting, leadership in project management, and collaborating with IT teams on technology strategy and integration. The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer will act as a key technical expert in designing and implementing complex desktop solutions across the enterprise. Pay range for the role is $140,000 to $150,000, with an annual bonus. The role can be 100% remote anywhere in United States.

Responsibilities

Advanced Desktop Infrastructure Management:Architect, deploy and maintain the organization's desktop environments, including Windows-based physical and virtual desktops (VDI).Develop and manage automated desktop deployments using tools such as Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) or equivalent technologies.Design, implement, and maintain desktop images, ensuring they are optimized for performance and security.Leadership and Strategy:Lead desktop infrastructure projects, such as OS migrations, VDI implementations, and major software rollouts.Provide mentorship and technical guidance to junior engineers and support staff.Collaborate with senior IT leadership to develop and implement long-term desktop technology strategies.Security and Compliance:Ensure compliance with internal security policies, industry standards, and audit requirements.Lead initiatives to enhance desktop security, including endpoint protection, encryption, and vulnerability management.Manage group policies, endpoint security software, and patch management processes using WSUS or other tools.Troubleshooting and Escalation:Serve as the highest escalation point for complex desktop-related issues, providing Level 3 support.Perform in-depth root cause analysis for recurring or complex technical problems and implement preventative measures.Provide expert support for remote access solutions, VPNs, and cloud desktop services.Automation and Efficiency:Develop and implement automation scripts using PowerShell and other tools to streamline desktop management tasks.Continuously seek opportunities to improve end-user computing efficiency, including desktop performance tuning and reducing downtime.System Integration:Collaborate with server, network, and security teams to ensure the seamless integration of desktop systems into the broader IT infrastructure.Ensure optimal configuration and performance of desktop environments within cloud services like Microsoft 365 and Azure Active Directory.Manage and integrate virtualization technologies (e.g., VMware Horizon, Citrix, Hyper-V).Documentation and Knowledge Sharing:Develop and maintain comprehensive documentation for desktop systems, policies, and procedures.Ensure knowledge sharing and training for IT staff on new technologies, best practices, and troubleshooting techniques.Actively participate in developing and updating standard operating procedures (SOPs) for desktop support.
Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent experience).5+ years of experience in desktop engineering or support, with significant experience in enterprise-level desktop management.Demonstrated experience leading major desktop infrastructure projects, including large-scale deployments, migrations, and VDI implementation.Expertise in Microsoft technologies, including SCCM, Intune, and Active Directory.Advanced knowledge of Windows 10/11, Group Policy, and desktop management tools.Strong PowerShell scripting skills for automation and troubleshooting.Experience with virtualization technologies (e.g., VMware, Citrix, Hyper-V) and virtual desktop environments (VDI).Familiarity with cloud-based services and integration (Microsoft 365, Azure AD, etc.).Strong leadership and team collaboration skills.Ability to manage multiple priorities and projects in a fast-paced environment.Excellent problem-solving and critical thinking abilities.Outstanding communication skills, both written and verbal.Certifications, Licenses, and/or RegistrationMicrosoft Certified: Modern Desktop Administrator ExpertMicrosoft Certified: Azure Fundamentals or Administrator AssociateVMware VCP or Citrix certifications.ITIL Certification or other relevant service management certifications.Physical Demands and Work EnvironmentThe physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit and use hands to handle, touch or feel objects, tools, or controls. The employee frequently is required to talk and hear. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate. The employee is occasionally required to stand; walk; reach with hands and arms. The employee is rarely required to stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl. The employee must regularly lift and/or move up to 10 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, color vision, and the ability to adjust focus.

jobs.description:
Job Summary

Reporting to the Manager of Digital Workplace Services, the Digital Workplace Engineer is responsible for supporting and maintaining core workplace technologies that enable productivity and collaboration. This role focuses on day-to-day operational tasks, basic troubleshooting, and assisting with system deployments under supervision.

Primary Responsibilities

Assist in deploying and configuring workplace technologies (e.g., Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive ,Outlook, and end point devices).Provide Service Desk support via ticketing system for digital workplace issues, escalating as needed.Maintain user accounts, permissions, and access controls.Contribute to documentation and knowledge sharing to enable consistent support and onboarding.Supports a collaborative relationship with other teams regarding patching and updates for workplace systems, excluding server infrastructure; may include mobile device management (MDM) platforms.Assist with technology transitions (e.g., domain registrar transfers, DNS updates, vendor coordination, and email migrations) to support organizational growth, acquisitions, and change.Support email migration tasks, such as mailbox setup and user onboarding.Perform basic administrative tasks in Microsoft Entra, including user provisioning, group membership updates, and license assignments.Implement and support automations by creating scripts and leveraging APIs to streamline existing manual processes; familiarity with using AI tools to accomplish dev work.Collaborate with senior engineers on project tasks.Follow security and compliance protocols.Perform other duties as assigned.Participate in rotating on call as needed.

Travel Required

Intermittent, less than 10%.

Essential Functions

The following duties and responsibilities generally reflect the expectations of this job but are not intended to be all-inclusive. The essential functions include the most significant tasks and are the essence of why the role exists; removing an essential duty would fundamentally alter the role.

Maintain and uphold the Core Values and Mission Statement of Mission Pet Health.Ensure reliable operation and support of digital workplace technologies to maintain employee productivity and collaboration.Provide prompt and effective Tier 1 support for workplace technology issues, ensuring positive user experiences and minimal downtime.Maintain accurate user account provisioning and access controls to uphold security and compliance standards.Uphold organizational policies, values, and security protocols in all work activities.Communicate effectively with team members and internal customers to resolve issues and support projects.Assist in after-hours maintenance windows to update, change, and install various systems as needed.Engage in project work including implementation of various solutions.Work with senior engineers on submitting proper change management requests to be reviewed by the change management team twice per week.

Additional Functions

Assist with training internal customers on Mission Pet Health approved technologies.Assist and participate in cross-functional teams and projects.Perform other related duties as assigned.

Required Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities

The knowledge, skills, and abilities required to be able to perform the Essential Functions of this position.

Familiarity with Microsoft 365 suite, including Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, Outlook, and Exchange Online.Basic understanding of Microsoft Entra (formerly Azure Active Directory) for user provisioning and access management.Knowledge of endpoint device setup and support (Windows, macOS, mobile).Basic troubleshooting skills for network connectivity, application access, and user experience issues.Strong customer service and communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical users.Organizational skills to manage multiple tasks and prioritize effectively.Attention to detail in maintaining user accounts, permissions, and compliance standards.Ability to document procedures and contribute to knowledge base articles.Ability to work collaboratively with senior engineers and cross-functional teams.Ability to learn new technologies quickly and adapt to changing environments.Ability to follow established security and compliance protocols.Ability to participate in after-hours maintenance windows when required.Ability to implement and support automations by creating scripts and leveraging APIs to streamline existing manual processes; familiarity with using AI tools to accomplish dev work.Ability to travel occasionally to support remote sites (less than 10%).

Required Education And Experience

The education level and amount of experience required to be able to perform the Essential Functions. This includes any required certifications and licenses.

Associate’s degree in information technology, Computer Science, or a related field.Will accept any equivalent combination of education, training, or experience.1-2 years of experience in IT support, help desk, or digital workplace technologies.Experience with Microsoft 365 (Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, Outlook) and basic user administration in Microsoft Entra is required.

Preferred Education And Experience

Bachelor’s degree in information technology or related major.Exposure to mobile device management (MDM) platforms such as Intune.Understanding of DNS concepts and domain registrar processes.Awareness of email migration tools and processes (e.g., cutover, staged, hybrid migrations).Experience with automation tooling and API-driven integrations (e.g., Microsoft Graph) and familiarity with AI-assisted development tools.

Physical Requirements

Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

How You're Supported

As part of the Mission Pet Health Home Office team, you'll be joining a collaborative group of professionals dedicated to supporting our hospital teams and shaping the future of veterinary medicine.

Competitive Compensation

A competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package.

Total Wellbeing

Comprehensive coverage including Health, Dental, and Vision insurance, plus 24/7 access to doctors through Teladoc.

Financial Health

Plan for your future with a 401k featuring a generous company match, plus Life Insurance and Short-Term Disability options.

Commitment to Growth

Benefit from significant opportunities for upward mobility and career growth within a rapidly expanding organization.

A Collaborative Culture

Join a nationwide community of over 20,000 veterinary professionals and a Home Office team that lives by our WAG values.

Valuable Perks

Enjoy generous paid time off and company-wide holidays, discounted veterinary care for your pets, and access to an online discount platform.

About Mission Pet Health

Mission Pet Health was founded by veterinarians on the belief that local practices thrive with clinical autonomy, collaboration, and medical excellence. The organization provides world-class support to its 850+ hospital teams on a hyper-local basis so they can focus on what matters most: the medicine, their patients, and their team culture. Learn more at missionpethealth.com.

Driving the award-winning culture are the WAG values - Work Together, Amaze, and Grow. Mission Pet Health is veterinarian-led, people-focused, and on a mission to be the best place to work in veterinary medicine.

EEO Statement: Mission Pet Health is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Reasonable Accommodations

Applicants with disabilities may be entitled to reasonable accommodation under the Americans with Disabilities Act and certain state or local laws. If you need a reasonable accommodation in order to perform the essential functions of a position, please send an e-mail to benefits@mvetpartners.com and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

Cash range $179K – $211K • Offers Equity • This role is also eligible for medical benefits, 401(k) plan, and other company perk programs.

At Vanta, our mission is to help businesses earn and prove trust. We believe that security should be monitored and verified continuously, and we empower companies to practice better security and prove it with ease. Vanta has a kind and talented team, and while some have prior security experience, many have been successful at Vanta without it.

As an IT Sr. Systems Engineer in the Corporate Engineering division, you will build and scale the systems, workflows, and automations that power the employee experience at Vanta. This is an opportunity to shape the future of enterprise tooling at a high-growth company, architecting decisions, developing scalable solutions, and enabling Vanta'ns to do their best work securely and efficiently.

The Corporate Engineering team is an employee-facing team with a mission to provide Vanta'ns with a secure and stellar experience with the tools and technologies they need to do their best work, regardless of where they are. This role will partner closely with Security, IT Operations, and cross-functional business stakeholders to design and maintain scalable corporate systems and integrations across Vanta's collaboration and productivity ecosystem.

What you’ll do as a Senior Systems Engineer at Vanta:

Design, deploy, and maintain business-critical corporate systems and SaaS platforms.

Build and configure AI-driven workflows and automations using tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and Dust, as well as custom development where needed.

Deploy and integrate AI capabilities into internal systems such as Slack and Okta using platforms like Vercel, to automate IT operations tasks and internal support, while maintaining security, privacy, and data integrity.

Own and improve workflows, automations, and integrations across collaboration and productivity tooling.

Partner with Security and IT Operations teams to implement and enforce policies, access controls, and compliance requirements across corporate systems.

Configure and support SSO, SCIM provisioning, and identity lifecycle integrations across applications.

Serve as an escalation point for complex systems and platform issues, while contributing to the team's shared ticket queue, which can make up a significant portion of the role.

Translate business requirements into scalable and maintainable technical solutions.

Create and maintain technical documentation, standards, and operational procedures.

Drive platform governance and best practices across enterprise tooling.

Collaborate with vendors on escalations, roadmap discussions, and technical troubleshooting.

How to be successful in this role:

Advanced proficiency administering a broad SaaS portfolio in hybrid, high-growth environments, with deep expertise in our core stack: Okta (including Workflows), Google Workspace, Slack Enterprise, and Atlassian.

Hands-on experience building integrations and automations against SaaS APIs, using scripting languages such as Python, Bash, or GAM.

Strong understanding of identity and access management concepts including SAML, OAuth, OIDC, and SCIM.

Proven ability to design scalable workflows and operational processes in enterprise environments.

Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills across interconnected systems.

Comfortable operating autonomously and driving projects from conception through implementation.

Effective communicator who can break down complex technical problems for non-technical stakeholders.

Write clear, maintainable documentation as a standard part of the work

Open to using AI to amplify their skills and strengthen their work - demonstrating curiosity, a willingness to learn, and sound judgment in applying AI responsibly to improve efficiency and impact.

Must be authorized to work in the U.S. without the need for current or future employer sponsorship

What you can expect as a Vanta’n:

Industry-competitive salary and equity

Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage, with 100% of employee-only benefit premiums covered for most medical plans

16 weeks paid Parental Leave for all new parents

Health & wellness stipend

Remote workspace, internet, and cellphone stipend

Commuter benefits for team members who report to the SF and NYC office

Family planning benefits

Matching 401(k) contribution with immediate vesting

Flexible PTO policy, plus 80 hours of Sick Time

11 company-paid holidays

Virtual team building activities, lunch and learns, and other company-wide events!

Offices in SF, NYC, London, Dublin, Tel Aviv, and Sydney

To provide greater transparency to candidates, we share base pay ranges for all US-based job postings regardless of state. We set standard base pay ranges for all roles based on function, level, and country location, benchmarked against similar-stage growth companies. Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors and may vary based on candidate location, skills, depth of work experience, and relevant licenses/credentials.

#LI-remote

At Vanta, we are committed to hiring diverse talent of different backgrounds and as such, it is important to us to provide an inclusive work environment for all. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, gender identity, age, religion, sexual orientation, veteran or disability status, or any other protected class. As an equal opportunity employer, we encourage and welcome people of all backgrounds to apply.

About Vanta

We started in 2018, in the wake of several high-profile data breaches. Online security was only becoming more important, but we knew firsthand how hard it could be for fast-growing companies to invest the time and manpower it takes to build a solid security foundation. Vanta was inspired by a vision to restore trust in internet businesses by enabling companies to improve and prove their security. From our early days automating security monitoring for compliance standards like SOC 2, HIPAA and ISO 27001 to creating the world's leading Trust Management Platform, our vision remains unchanged.

Now more than ever, making security continuous—not just a point-in-time check— is essential. Thousands of companies rely on Vanta to build, maintain and demonstrate their trust— all in a way that's real-time and transparent.

Referral Instructions

If you are being referred for the role, please contact that person to apply on your behalf.

jobs.description:
Description:The Responsible Systems Engineer (RSE) for the FBM Program Boost Control System (PBCS) serves as the single technical point of contact for the entire PBCS suite and each constituent product, providing continuity of product knowledge, source data, and traceability of all system relationships, owners, and required accountabilities. Acting as a knowledgeable, experienced, and trained specialist, the RSE advocates for the product throughout its lifecycle—from requirements definition through mission operations—and leads the life‑extension effort to ensure sustained reliability, availability, and performance. In this role the engineer collaborates with software, hardware, configuration‑management, test, logistics, and program‑management teams to develop integration strategies, conduct risk‑based assessments, and implement technical improvements across PBCS components. The RSE is accountable for proper performance and mission success of the PBCS, resisting inappropriate compromises, balancing technical performance against cost and schedule pressures, and ensuring that all engineering changes support the system’s extended service life while maintaining overall mission readiness.

As a valued member of our team you will:

Report to the FBM Controls PBCS Manager/ FBM Chief Systems Engineer and is responsible for providing technical oversight of the Trident II (D5LE) PBCS and Mission Success throughout the life cycle of the missile sub-system.

Is the technical expert who maintains a full understanding of the D5LE PBCS and verifies compliance with sub system requirements.

Is the technical advocate for the D5LE PBCS who gathers performance information, supports major reviews, and participates in critical production, critical missile design decisions and alteration activities to support overall missile system assessments.

Participates in problem investigations, troubleshooting, development of corrective action plans, and integration of technical requirements with facility and fleet operation activities.

Integrate new development efforts into SWF operations and capabilities to support new fleet deployments.

Is responsible for having a complete understanding of program-unique and Space command media which ensures the PBCS meet technical requirements throughout their lifecycle.

Is accountable for the technical integrity of the D5 PBCS. This includes ensuring compatibility between internal interfaces and across interfacing subsystems of the Strategic Weapon System.
Basic Qualifications:
Experience working with Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) and other technical personnel to create program plans and track the plans through execution

Experience working with system and/or program requirements on cross functional teams

Experience successfully leading projects with teams and suppliers located across multiple locations

Experience with understanding customer needs, such as participating in Customer Value Propositions

Experience putting together presentations to internal senior leaders such as Directors

Active or ability to gain a DoD security clearance
Desired Skills:
- Missile and/or aerospace related education or experience

- Missile and/or space propulsion hardware experience
- Experience with FBM processes and products
- Experience with aerospace manufacturing
- Experience in gas/fluid dynamics or energetics/propellants
- Experience as principal engineer delegate or project lead
- Risk and opportunity experience with an understanding of continuous improvement concepts
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills, demonstrated leadership and initiative
Security Clearance Statement: This position requires a government security clearance, you must be a US Citizen for consideration.
Clearance Level: Secret
Other Important Information You Should Know
Expression of Interest: By applying to this job, you are expressing interest in this position and could be considered for other career opportunities where similar skills and requirements have been identified as a match. Should this match be identified you may be contacted for this and future openings.
Ability to Work Remotely: Onsite Full-time: The work associated with this position will be performed onsite at a designated Lockheed Martin facility.
Work Schedules: Lockheed Martin supports a variety of alternate work schedules that provide additional flexibility to our employees. Schedules range from standard 40 hours over a five day work week while others may be condensed. These condensed schedules provide employees with additional time away from the office and are in addition to our Paid Time off benefits.
Schedule for this Position: 9x80 every other Friday off
Pay Rate: The annual base salary range for this position in California, Massachusetts, and New York (excluding most major metropolitan areas), Colorado, Hawaii, Illinois, Maryland, Minnesota, New Jersey, Vermont, Washington or Washington DC is $104,500 - $184,115. For states not referenced above, the salary range for this position will reflect the candidate’s final work location. Please note that the salary information is a general guideline only. Lockheed Martin considers factors such as (but not limited to) scope and responsibilities of the position, candidate's work experience, education/ training, key skills as well as market and business considerations when extending an offer.
Benefits offered: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, Short-Term Disability, Long-Term Disability, 401(k) match, Flexible Spending Accounts, EAP, Education Assistance, Parental Leave, Paid time off, and Holidays.
(Washington state applicants only) Non-represented full-time employees: accrue at least 10 hours per month of Paid Time Off (PTO) to be used for incidental absences and other reasons; receive at least 90 hours for holidays. Represented full time employees accrue 6.67 hours of Vacation per month; accrue up to 52 hours of sick leave annually; receive at least 96 hours for holidays. PTO, Vacation, sick leave, and holiday hours are prorated based on start date during the calendar year.
This position is incentive plan eligible.
Lockheed Martin is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified candidates will be considered without regard to legally protected characteristics.
The application window will close in 90 days; applicants are encouraged to apply within 5 - 30 days of the requisition posting date in order to receive optimal consideration.

Description:
Are you passionate about serving patients in an inspiring work environment alongside talented people? Get moving with OCR! With over 50 years in business, we are the premier provider of orthopedic care in Colorado, Wyoming, and Nebraska. Our organization continues to grow, and we are looking for individuals who share our mission.

The Systems Engineer- Level II is responsible for the maintenance, configuration, and reliable operation of OCR computer systems. This position will install hardware and software to end users, manage various systems and ensure minimal downtime and efficient operations. They also will participate in system testing and recommendations for system improvements.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities

Performs full lifecycle of installation, management, maintenance, and disposal of workstations and laptops.
Manages users in Active Directory and Microsoft Azure.
Manage Microsoft E365 tenant account, including user account license assignments, Exchange Online mailboxes, Teams, OneDrive and SharePoint files, and Intune.
Designs, tests and implements Active Directory Group Purchasing Organizations (GPOs) in coordination with System Architect.
Recommends and implements cybersecurity policies and practices, in coordination with System Architect.
Performs system maintenance and patching regularly within the respective change windows.
Maintains adequate clone images and spare workstations for rapid deployment and replacement.
Installs and configures hardware in various company locations; revises processes and procedures as needed.
Participates in the IT change management process, and adheres to it.
Maintains system and application diagrams and documentation.
Participates in daily helpdesk ticket resolution.
Participates in IT on-call rotation.
Participates in tier 1 support for Epic electronic health record
Other duties as assigned.
Requirements:

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in computer science, information systems, or related field, required.
Two years’ experience with active directory management.
Two years’ experience with Microsoft operating systems and Microsoft Office suites.
Experience with software maintenance and testing.
Additional education may be substituted for experience.

Physical Requirements

The work environment is a typical medical office environment. The employee must be able to complete their work satisfactorily in an environment where there are significant distractions, including staff, patients, and vendors walking through and conversing, telephones ringing, conversations carrying over, loud noises, and interruptions to answer questions from others.
While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit and use hands to finger, handle, or feel objects, tools, or controls. The employee frequently is required to talk or hear. The employee is frequently required to stand; walk; reach with hands and arms, and stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl.
The employee must regularly lift and/or move up to 25 pounds and should do so in a sound and safe manner. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus.

Employee Assistance Program

Company-sponsored events
Annual merit increases

jobs.description:
Job Details

Description

The Modern Workplace Engineer Level I engineer supports Microsoft 365 environments for our Defense Industrial Base customers. This individual is responsible for delivering secure, compliant, and efficient modern workplace experiences using the Microsoft 365 E5 suite.

This role will focus on both the ongoing operations of managed services as well as project-based onboarding and adoption.

Role & Responsibilities

End User Productivity Support

Provide Tier I support for Microsoft 365 applications, including: Exchange Online Microsoft Teams SharePoint Online OneDrive for Business Microsoft Office Apps (Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.) Escalate support requests as necessary to Level II engineers. Troubleshoot issues related to Microsoft 365 access, synchronization, and collaboration.

Identity & Access Support

Configure and maintain Conditional Access, passwordless authentication, and identity protection policies in Microsoft Entra ID. Support hybrid identity scenarios and secure guest access across Microsoft 365 services.

Security & Compliance Tasks

Assist with reviewing and applying sensitivity labels, retention policies, and data loss prevention (DLP) rules within Microsoft Purview (Compliance Center). Monitor alerts in Microsoft Defender for Office 365 and escalate issues per defined procedures.

Service Delivery & Operations

Respond to service requests and incidents in alignment with SLAs and internal escalation paths. Perform basic configuration and policy updates using Microsoft 365 Admin Center and Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune). Follow internal processes (such as change controls) for making changes in customer environments. Document technical issues, solutions, and recurring patterns in internal knowledge base systems.

Customer Engagement

Work directly with customer stakeholders, primarily technical contacts, to resolve routine issues and implement M365 changes. Participate in onboarding activities for new users, sites, or departments within existing managed customers.

Team Collaboration

Coordinate with higher-level engineers to escalate complex issues or contribute to larger projects. Participate in regular team syncs and ongoing training sessions to stay current on Microsoft 365 features and changes. Coordinate with Endpoint Engineering, Security Operations, and Azure Engineering teams to resolve issues.

Competencies / Skills

1–2 years of IT support experience, preferably with a managed service provider (MSP) or in a customer-facing technical role. Working knowledge of Microsoft 365 administration portals (Admin Center, Teams Admin Center, Exchange Admin Center). Strong troubleshooting skills and attention to detail. jobs.description:
Your Impact

At Goldman Sachs, Workplace Engineering is at the forefront of driving our firm's adoption of cutting-edge technologies for productivity, communications, and collaboration. As a Senior Technical Vice President, you will play a pivotal leadership role in shaping the strategic direction, architecture, and operational excellence of our Microsoft 365 platforms. Your vision and leadership will directly influence how our global workforce interacts, collaborates, and innovates, ensuring robust, secure, and scalable solutions that underpin our business objectives and client engagement. You will lead a high-performing engineering organization, driving innovation in web, mobile, social, collaboration, and user experience, fundamentally changing the way we operate across the firm.

Role Summary

This role is a Microsoft 365 Engineer with a core focus on Microsoft 365 engineering, Intune (mobile application and device management), M365 platform readiness, extensibility, security, and enterprise-scale adoption. We are seeking a visionary and experienced technical leader to serve as our Senior Technical Vice President, overseeing the strategic planning, engineering, and governance of our enterprise-wide Microsoft 365 ecosystem. This role demands a deep technical understanding of M365 services, coupled with exceptional leadership capabilities to guide global teams, define architectural standards, and drive the adoption of advanced features, including AI-powered capabilities like Copilot, the Teams suite, and the Power Platform. You will be instrumental in collaborating with senior product owners, architects, compliance, and risk teams to deliver transformative solutions that meet the stringent demands of a global financial institution, ensuring seamless integration across the broader Microsoft ecosystem including Windows 365.

Responsibilities

* Define and execute the long-term strategic vision and roadmap for all Microsoft 365 platforms and services, ensuring alignment with overall firm objectives, technological advancements, and regulatory requirements.

* Lead, mentor, and develop a global team of highly skilled engineers and technical managers, fostering a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement.

* Provide executive oversight for the design, architecture, and implementation of complex, enterprise-grade Microsoft 365 solutions, ensuring scalability, security, resilience, and seamless integration with existing firm-wide systems, including Windows 365 and the Power Platform.

* Establish and enforce robust governance frameworks, security policies, and compliance standards for all Microsoft 365 services, ensuring adherence to internal policies and external regulatory mandates within a highly regulated financial services environment.

* Collaborate extensively with senior product owners, enterprise architects, engineering leads, compliance, risk, and business unit leaders to understand strategic requirements, manage expectations, and drive successful outcomes for critical initiatives.

* Drive the evaluation, pilot, and strategic adoption of new Microsoft 365 features, emerging technologies, and AI-powered capabilities (e.g., Copilot, Teams, Power Platform) to enhance productivity, collaboration, and user experience across the firm.

* Oversee the operational, performance, and incident management processes for Microsoft 365 environments, serving as a primary executive escalation point for critical issues and ensuring rapid resolution and root cause analysis.

Basic Qualifications

* 12-15+ years of progressive experience in enterprise-level IT, with at least 10 years specifically focused on Microsoft 365 platforms and services in a significant leadership capacity within a large, complex organization.

* Proven track record of successfully leading, managing, and developing large, geographically dispersed technical teams, including managers and individual contributors.

* Demonstrated ability to define, articulate, and execute complex technology strategies and

roadmaps for critical enterprise collaboration platforms.

* Deep architectural and engineering understanding of the entire Microsoft 365 suite, including Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Teams, OneDrive, Azure AD, Intune, Purview, Windows 365, and the Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI), as well as advanced security/compliance features.

* Extensive experience designing, implementing, and managing Microsoft 365 solutions within stringent security, governance, and regulatory frameworks, preferably in the financial services industry.

* Exceptional executive-level communication, presentation, and negotiation skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts and strategic initiatives to diverse audiences, including senior leadership and non-technical stakeholders.

* Superior analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities, capable of navigating highly complex technical, organizational, and business challenges.

Preferred Qualifications

* Hands-on experience with large-scale Microsoft 365 Copilot deployments, Power Platform integrations, custom integrations, and advanced automation strategies for M365 management and operations.

* Strong understanding of broader cloud architecture principles (Azure, AWS, GCP) and experience managing hybrid cloud environments.

* Relevant industry certifications such as Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Microsoft 365 Certified: Enterprise Administrator Expert, or similar.

* Prior experience in a senior technology leadership role within a global financial institution, with a strong understanding of industry-specific challenges and regulations.

* Proficiency in advanced PowerShell scripting, Azure Automation, and other automation frameworks for M365 management and operations.

Salary Range The expected base salary for this Seattle, Washington, United States-based position is $150000-$250000. In addition, you may be eligible for a discretionary bonus if you are an active employee as of fiscal year-end.

Benefits Goldman Sachs is committed to providing our people with valuable and competitive benefits and wellness offerings, as it is a core part of providing a strong overall employee experience.
